description_long: This is a partially constructed wooden house with a visible timber frame structure and exposed beams, suggesting ongoing construction or a cutaway architectural model. The house features multiple gabled roofs, with the white framing clearly outlining the roof trusses and support beams. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, and several white-framed windows are symmetrically distributed across the facade. The structure includes a prominent central section flanked by two side wings, and a covered porch or entryway supported by posts. The house appears to have a single main story with a high-pitched roof, and the open framework reveals the internal room divisions. The overall design is reminiscent of a Craftsman or traditional American suburban home, with a focus on wood construction and geometric rooflines.
description: A partially constructed wooden house with exposed white roof framing, horizontal wood siding, and symmetrically placed white-framed windows. The structure features multiple gabled roofs and a central entryway with a covered porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, central gable, and symmetrical window arrangement, with exposed roof framing and a covered porch area.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the house, the high-pitched gabled roof, and the arrangement of windows along the side wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back facade, additional windows, and the continuation of the exposed roof trusses and beams.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the extended porch structure, multiple windows, and the full span of the roof framework.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entryway with a covered porch, supported by posts and framed by a prominent gable, featuring a standard door and decorative trim.
